content:
You are a programming expert. 
You will be provided with a patch used to fix a bug. 

Your task is summarize the modifications made in the patch.
Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From 5bc4e6d7b1c22dc1bf99f475b7e70594ebdd83b9 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001 From: Mark Thomas Date: Wed, 3 Apr 2019 17:00:38 +0200 Subject: [PATCH] Limit CGI command line arguments Limit the decoded form of individual command line arguments. This is to work various issues passing command line arguments from Java to the OS on Windows. This restriction may be overridden by the new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsDecoded. This is the fix for CVE-2019-0232. diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties index 0714ad8862..2edd6bfdad 100644 --- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties +++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties @@ -23,6 +23,7 @@ cgiServlet.expandOk=Expanded script at path [{0}] to [{1}] cgiServlet.find.found=Found CGI: name [{0}], path [{1}], script name [{2}] and CGI name [{3}] cgiServlet.find.location=Looking for a file at [{0}] cgiServlet.find.path=CGI script requested at path [{0}] relative to CGI location [{1}] +cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded=The decoded command line argument [{0}] did not match the configured cmdLineArgumentsDecoded pattern [{1}] cgiServlet.invalidArgumentEncoded=The encoded command line argument [{0}] did not match the configured cmdLineArgumentsEncoded pattern [{1}] cgiServlet.runBadHeader=Bad header line [{0}] cgiServlet.runFail=I/O problems processing CGI diff --git a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml index af1e1e23a1..79610111cf 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml @@ -103,6 +103,14 @@ 3875. This restriction may be relaxed by the use of the new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsEncoded. (markt) + + When the CGI Servlet is configured with + enableCmdLineArguments set to true, limit the decoded form + of the individual command line arguments to known safe values when + running on Windows. This restriction may be relaxed by the use of the + new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsDecoded. This + is the fix for CVE-2019-0232. (markt) + diff --git a/conf/web.xml b/conf/web.xml index f16be3b1b1..0a348386a6 100644 --- a/conf/web.xml +++ b/conf/web.xml @@ -335,6 +335,20 @@ + + + + + + + + + + + + + + diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java index 15a2bd4cc9..56d912ca90 100644 --- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java +++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java @@ -52,6 +52,7 @@ import javax.servlet.http.HttpSession; import org.apache.catalina.util.IOTools; import org.apache.juli.logging.Log; import org.apache.juli.logging.LogFactory; +import org.apache.tomcat.util.compat.JrePlatform; import org.apache.tomcat.util.res.StringManager; @@ -245,10 +246,21 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L; private static final Set DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS = new HashSet<>(); + private static final Pattern DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + private static final String ALLOW_ANY_PATTERN = ".*"; + static { DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("HEAD"); DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("OPTIONS"); DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("TRACE"); + + if (JrePlatform.IS_WINDOWS) { + DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("[a-zA-Z0-9\\Q-_.\\/:\\E]+"); + } else { + // No restrictions + DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN = null; + } + } @@ -314,6 +326,14 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsEncodedPattern = Pattern.compile("[a-zA-Z0-9\\Q%;/?:@&,$-_.!~*'()\\E]+"); + /** + * Limits the decoded form of individual command line arguments. Default + * varies by platform. + */ + private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + + + /** * Sets instance variables. *

@@ -411,6 +431,14 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { cmdLineArgumentsEncodedPattern = Pattern.compile(getServletConfig().getInitParameter("cmdLineArgumentsEncoded")); } + + String value = getServletConfig().getInitParameter("cmdLineArgumentsDecoded"); + if (ALLOW_ANY_PATTERN.equals(value)) { + // Optimisation for case where anything is allowed + cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = null; + } else if (value != null) { + cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = Pattern.compile(value); + } } @@ -792,7 +820,17 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { } return false; } + String decodedArgument = URLDecoder.decode(encodedArgument, parameterEncoding); + if (cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern != null && + !cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern.matcher(decodedArgument).matches()) { + if (log.isDebugEnabled()) { + log.debug(sm.getString("cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded", + decodedArgument, cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern.toString())); + } + return false; + } + cmdLineParameters.add(decodedArgument); } } @@ -1101,7 +1139,6 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { this.env = envp; return true; - } /** diff --git a/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml b/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml index 92a94289ab..b368f3c541 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml @@ -102,12 +102,22 @@ the web application root directory + File.separator + this prefix. By default there is no value, which results in the web application root directory being used as the search path. The recommended value is WEB-INF/cgi +

  • cmdLineArgumentsDecoded - If command line argumemnts +are enabled (via enableCmdLineArguments) and Tomcat is running +on Windows then each individual decoded command line argument must match this +pattern else the request will be rejected. This is to protect against known +issues passing command line arguments from Java to Windows. These issues can +lead to remote code execution. For more information on these issues see +Markus +Wulftange's blog and this archived +blog +by Daniel Colascione.
  • cmdLineArgumentsEncoded - If command line argumemnts are enabled (via enableCmdLineArguments) individual encoded command line argument must match this pattern else the request will be rejected. The default matches the allowed values defined by RFC3875 and is [a-zA-Z0-9\Q%;/?:@&,$-_.!~*'()\E]+
    • -
  • enableCmdLineArguments - Are command line parameters +
  • enableCmdLineArguments - Are command line arguments generated from the query string as per section 4.4 of 3875 RFC? The default is true.
  • environment-variable- - An environment to be set for the diff --git a/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml b/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml index 7a56bf1518..71358de4cd 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml @@ -507,6 +507,14 @@ initialisation parameter should not be set to 10 or higher on a production system because the debug page is not secure.

    +

    When using the CGI Servlet on Windows with + enableCmdLineArguments enabled, review the setting of + cmdLineArgumentsDecoded carefully and ensure that it is + appropriate for your environment. The default value is secure. Insecure + configurations may expose the server to remote code execution. Further + information on the potential risks and mitigations may be found by + following the links in the CGI How To.

    +

    FailedRequestFilter can be configured and used to reject requests that had errors during request parameter parsing. Without the filter the default behaviour is -- 2.34.1

### Modifications in Each File

1. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties`**
   - Added a new string entry: `cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded`, which provides a message for when a decoded command line argument does not match the configured pattern.

2. **`webapps/docs/changelog.xml`**
   - Added a new entry in the changelog detailing the new restriction on decoded command line arguments when the CGI Servlet is configured with `enableCmdLineArguments` set to true, specifically for Windows. It mentions the new initialization parameter `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` and references CVE-2019-0232.

3. **`conf/web.xml`**
   - Added comments explaining the new initialization parameter `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded`, including its purpose, default values based on the platform, and its role in mitigating issues with command line arguments on Windows.

4. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java`**
   - Introduced a new pattern `cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern` to limit the decoded form of command line arguments based on the platform (Windows vs. others).
   - Updated the initialization logic to set this pattern based on the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter.
   - Added validation logic to check if the decoded command line arguments match the specified pattern, logging a debug message if they do not.

5. **`webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml`**
   - Updated documentation to include details about the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, its purpose, and the security implications of its configuration on Windows.

6. **`webapps/docs/security-howto.xml`**
   - Added a paragraph advising users to review the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` setting when using the CGI Servlet on Windows, highlighting the potential security risks and linking to further information.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modifications collectively address security concerns related to command line arguments passed from Java to the operating system, particularly on Windows. The introduction of the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter allows for a configurable pattern that individual decoded command line arguments must match, thereby mitigating risks of remote code execution (CVE-2019-0232). 

The changes in the `CGIServlet.java` file implement the logic to enforce this new restriction, while the updates in the properties and documentation files provide necessary context and guidance for users configuring the servlet. The changelog and documentation updates ensure that users are informed about the new features and their implications, promoting secure configurations.

Based on your summary and the commit message in the patch, you need to:

1. analyze the root cause of the bug in detail. 
2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug. 



Provide a response strictly in the following format:
Root cause:<Root cause>
file:<file name>

Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From 5bc4e6d7b1c22dc1bf99f475b7e70594ebdd83b9 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001 From: Mark Thomas Date: Wed, 3 Apr 2019 17:00:38 +0200 Subject: [PATCH] Limit CGI command line arguments Limit the decoded form of individual command line arguments. This is to work various issues passing command line arguments from Java to the OS on Windows. This restriction may be overridden by the new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsDecoded. This is the fix for CVE-2019-0232. diff --git a/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml b/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml index 92a94289ab..b368f3c541 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml @@ -102,12 +102,22 @@ the web application root directory + File.separator + this prefix. By default there is no value, which results in the web application root directory being used as the search path. The recommended value is WEB-INF/cgi +
  • cmdLineArgumentsDecoded - If command line argumemnts +are enabled (via enableCmdLineArguments) and Tomcat is running +on Windows then each individual decoded command line argument must match this +pattern else the request will be rejected. This is to protect against known +issues passing command line arguments from Java to Windows. These issues can +lead to remote code execution. For more information on these issues see +Markus +Wulftange's blog and this archived +blog +by Daniel Colascione.
  • cmdLineArgumentsEncoded - If command line argumemnts are enabled (via enableCmdLineArguments) individual encoded command line argument must match this pattern else the request will be rejected. The default matches the allowed values defined by RFC3875 and is [a-zA-Z0-9\Q%;/?:@&,$-_.!~*'()\E]+
    • -
  • enableCmdLineArguments - Are command line parameters +
  • enableCmdLineArguments - Are command line arguments generated from the query string as per section 4.4 of 3875 RFC? The default is true.
  • environment-variable- - An environment to be set for the diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java index 15a2bd4cc9..56d912ca90 100644 --- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java +++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java @@ -52,6 +52,7 @@ import javax.servlet.http.HttpSession; import org.apache.catalina.util.IOTools; import org.apache.juli.logging.Log; import org.apache.juli.logging.LogFactory; +import org.apache.tomcat.util.compat.JrePlatform; import org.apache.tomcat.util.res.StringManager; @@ -245,10 +246,21 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L; private static final Set DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS = new HashSet<>(); + private static final Pattern DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + private static final String ALLOW_ANY_PATTERN = ".*"; + static { DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("HEAD"); DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("OPTIONS"); DEFAULT_SUPER_METHODS.add("TRACE"); + + if (JrePlatform.IS_WINDOWS) { + DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("[a-zA-Z0-9\\Q-_.\\/:\\E]+"); + } else { + // No restrictions + DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN = null; + } + } @@ -314,6 +326,14 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsEncodedPattern = Pattern.compile("[a-zA-Z0-9\\Q%;/?:@&,$-_.!~*'()\\E]+"); + /** + * Limits the decoded form of individual command line arguments. Default + * varies by platform. + */ + private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + + + /** * Sets instance variables. *

    @@ -411,6 +431,14 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { cmdLineArgumentsEncodedPattern = Pattern.compile(getServletConfig().getInitParameter("cmdLineArgumentsEncoded")); } + + String value = getServletConfig().getInitParameter("cmdLineArgumentsDecoded"); + if (ALLOW_ANY_PATTERN.equals(value)) { + // Optimisation for case where anything is allowed + cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = null; + } else if (value != null) { + cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = Pattern.compile(value); + } } @@ -792,7 +820,17 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { } return false; } + String decodedArgument = URLDecoder.decode(encodedArgument, parameterEncoding); + if (cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern != null && + !cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern.matcher(decodedArgument).matches()) { + if (log.isDebugEnabled()) { + log.debug(sm.getString("cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded", + decodedArgument, cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern.toString())); + } + return false; + } + cmdLineParameters.add(decodedArgument); } } @@ -1101,7 +1139,6 @@ public final class CGIServlet extends HttpServlet { this.env = envp; return true; - } /** diff --git a/conf/web.xml b/conf/web.xml index f16be3b1b1..0a348386a6 100644 --- a/conf/web.xml +++ b/conf/web.xml @@ -335,6 +335,20 @@ + + + + + + + + + + + + + + diff --git a/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml b/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml index 7a56bf1518..71358de4cd 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/security-howto.xml @@ -507,6 +507,14 @@ initialisation parameter should not be set to 10 or higher on a production system because the debug page is not secure.

    +

    When using the CGI Servlet on Windows with + enableCmdLineArguments enabled, review the setting of + cmdLineArgumentsDecoded carefully and ensure that it is + appropriate for your environment. The default value is secure. Insecure + configurations may expose the server to remote code execution. Further + information on the potential risks and mitigations may be found by + following the links in the CGI How To.

    +

    FailedRequestFilter can be configured and used to reject requests that had errors during request parameter parsing. Without the filter the default behaviour is -- 2.34.1 diff --git a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties index 0714ad8862..2edd6bfdad 100644 --- a/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties +++ b/java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties @@ -23,6 +23,7 @@ cgiServlet.expandOk=Expanded script at path [{0}] to [{1}] cgiServlet.find.found=Found CGI: name [{0}], path [{1}], script name [{2}] and CGI name [{3}] cgiServlet.find.location=Looking for a file at [{0}] cgiServlet.find.path=CGI script requested at path [{0}] relative to CGI location [{1}] +cgiServlet.invalidArgumentDecoded=The decoded command line argument [{0}] did not match the configured cmdLineArgumentsDecoded pattern [{1}] cgiServlet.invalidArgumentEncoded=The encoded command line argument [{0}] did not match the configured cmdLineArgumentsEncoded pattern [{1}] cgiServlet.runBadHeader=Bad header line [{0}] cgiServlet.runFail=I/O problems processing CGI diff --git a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml index af1e1e23a1..79610111cf 100644 --- a/webapps/docs/changelog.xml +++ b/webapps/docs/changelog.xml @@ -103,6 +103,14 @@ 3875. This restriction may be relaxed by the use of the new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsEncoded. (markt) + + When the CGI Servlet is configured with + enableCmdLineArguments set to true, limit the decoded form + of the individual command line arguments to known safe values when + running on Windows. This restriction may be relaxed by the use of the + new initialisation parameter cmdLineArgumentsDecoded. This + is the fix for CVE-2019-0232. (markt) +

### Modifications in Each File

1. **`webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml`**:
   - Added a new section describing the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, which limits the decoded form of individual command line arguments when enabled and Tomcat is running on Windows. This is to mitigate issues related to command line argument handling that could lead to remote code execution.

2. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java`**:
   - Introduced a new pattern `DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN` that restricts decoded command line arguments on Windows.
   - Added logic to set `cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern` based on the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` initialization parameter.
   - Implemented validation to check if decoded command line arguments match the specified pattern, logging an error if they do not.

3. **`conf/web.xml`**:
   - Added documentation for the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, explaining its purpose and default behavior across platforms.

4. **`webapps/docs/security-howto.xml`**:
   - Included a note about the importance of reviewing the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` setting when using the CGI Servlet on Windows, highlighting potential security risks.

5. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties`**:
   - Added a new string for logging invalid decoded command line arguments that do not match the configured pattern.

6. **`webapps/docs/changelog.xml`**:
   - Documented the new feature that limits the decoded command line arguments for the CGI Servlet on Windows, mentioning the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter and its relation to CVE-2019-0232.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modifications across the files are interconnected as they collectively address the security vulnerability CVE-2019-0232 related to command line argument handling in the CGI Servlet on Windows. The `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter is introduced to provide a configurable way to enforce restrictions on decoded command line arguments, enhancing security. The changes in the `CGIServlet.java` file implement the logic to enforce these restrictions, while the documentation updates in the XML files ensure that users are informed about the new parameter and its implications. The addition of logging for invalid arguments further aids in debugging and maintaining security compliance. Overall, these changes work together to improve the robustness of command line argument handling in the application.

Default + * varies by platform. + */ + private Pattern cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern = DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN; + + + /** * Sets instance variables. *

### Modifications in Each File

1. **`webapps/docs/security-howto.xml`**:
   - Added a paragraph explaining the importance of reviewing the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` setting when using the CGI Servlet on Windows. It highlights the potential security risks and provides links for further information.

2. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/CGIServlet.java`**:
   - Introduced a new pattern `DEFAULT_CMD_LINE_ARGUMENTS_DECODED_PATTERN` to limit the decoded form of command line arguments on Windows.
   - Added logic to set `cmdLineArgumentsDecodedPattern` based on the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` initialization parameter.
   - Implemented validation to check if decoded command line arguments match the specified pattern, logging a debug message if they do not.

3. **`java/org/apache/catalina/servlets/LocalStrings.properties`**:
   - Added a new string for logging invalid decoded command line arguments, providing a message format for debugging.

4. **`webapps/docs/cgi-howto.xml`**:
   - Updated documentation to include details about the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, explaining its purpose and the security implications of command line arguments on Windows.

5. **`webapps/docs/changelog.xml`**:
   - Added an entry to the changelog summarizing the new restriction on decoded command line arguments for the CGI Servlet on Windows and the introduction of the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter.

6. **`conf/web.xml`**:
   - Added comments to document the `cmdLineArgumentsDecoded` parameter, explaining its usage and the default patterns for Windows and other platforms.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modifications across the files are interconnected as they collectively address the security vulnerability CVE-2019-0232 related to command line arguments in the CGI Servlet on Windows. The changes in `CGIServlet.java` implement the core functionality to restrict and validate command line arguments, while the updates in documentation files (`security-howto.xml`, `cgi-howto.xml`, and `changelog.xml`) provide necessary context, usage instructions, and security implications for users. The addition of logging in `LocalStrings.properties` enhances the ability to debug issues related to invalid command line arguments. Overall, these changes work together to improve security and provide clear guidance on the new configuration options.
